description: Complete prompt engineering system — all tags, categories, detailed info, and saved prompts. Use this to generate structured prompts with XML tags following prompt engineering best practices.
instructions: When generating prompts, use this JSON as reference. Follow the tag structure, respect the recommended order (Identity → Context → Task → Constraints → Reasoning → Output → Control), and include the detail info (justification, useCases, recommendations, limitations) to make informed decisions about which tags to use.
recommended_tag_order: 1. Identity (role, persona, system, audience, domain, perspective)
2. Objective (task, goal, sub_tasks, success_criteria, scope, priority, instructions)
3. Knowledge (context, examples, reference, memory, knowledge_base, definitions, assumptions)
6. Cognition (reasoning, chain_of_thought, tree_of_thought, planning, reflection, verification, self_critique, metacognition, thinking)
7. Agentic (tools, tool_selection, action, observation, scratchpad, delegation, state)
8. Output (output, format, style, final_output, response_length, citations, language, reading_level)
9. Control (stop_condition, hitl_conditions, fallback, retry, conditional, loop, error_handling, handoff)
10. Safety (guardrails, validation, content_policy, pii_handling, injection_defense, ethical_guidelines, compliance)

tags.name: role
tags.description: Define the AI persona, expertise, and behavioral identity

detail.justification: Setting a role is the single most impactful prompt engineering technique. It primes the model's knowledge distribution and response patterns toward the specified expertise.
recommendations: Be specific: 'senior React developer with 10 years experience' > 'developer'
limitations: Role-playing can reduce the model's willingness to say 'I don't know'
Very niche roles may trigger hallucinated expertise
Does not override safety training

tags.name: persona
tags.description: Detailed character profile for the AI to adopt

detail.justification: Personas go deeper than roles — they define personality, communication style, quirks, and emotional tone, creating a consistent character across interactions.
useCases: Educational tutors with specific teaching styles
recommendations: Include name, background, communication style, and values
Define how the persona handles uncertainty or conflict
Use with <audience> to calibrate the persona's language
limitations: Complex personas increase token usage
May conflict with safety guidelines if persona is edgy
Hard to maintain consistency over very long conversations

tags.name: system
tags.description: System-level instructions (for chat API format)

detail.justification: Maps directly to the 'system' message role in chat APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). System messages receive elevated instruction priority in most models.
recommendations: Place your most critical instructions here
Keep focused — system message bloat reduces effectiveness
Use for behavioral rules that must persist across all turns
limitations: Not all models treat system messages with equal priority
Very long system messages can degrade performance
Some models may still override system instructions with clever user prompts

tags.name: audience
tags.description: Who the output is intended for (calibrates complexity and vocabulary)

detail.justification: Specifying the audience dramatically shapes vocabulary, depth, assumed knowledge, and explanation density.
useCases: Documentation generation for different skill levels
Educational content for specific grade levels
recommendations: Include knowledge assumptions: 'familiar with REST APIs but new to GraphQL'
limitations: Model may over-simplify for non-expert audiences
Multiple audiences in one prompt create conflict
Cultural assumptions may not transfer globally

tags.name: domain
tags.description: Specific professional/technical domain to operate within

detail.justification: Anchors terminology, conventions, standards, and best practices. A 'review' in medicine vs. software vs. law means entirely different things.
limitations: Very niche domains may have limited training data
Cross-domain tasks may create terminology conflicts
Domain expertise can increase hallucination confidence

tags.name: perspective
tags.description: Viewpoint or stance to adopt (e.g., devil's advocate, red-teamer)

detail.justification: Enables multi-perspective analysis, debate-style reasoning, and adversarial thinking without changing the core role.
limitations: Model may struggle to maintain perspective over long outputs
Extreme perspectives may trigger safety filters
Can confuse the model if combined with a contradictory role

tags.name: task
tags.description: Main task or objective for the AI to accomplish

detail.justification: The task is the central instruction. Without a clear task, models produce unfocused responses. It is the single most important section after role.
limitations: Overly complex single tasks reduce quality
Ambiguous tasks lead to hallucination
Multiple tasks in one tag can cause the model to skip some

tags.name: goal
tags.description: High-level desired outcome or success state

detail.justification: Distinguishes 'what success looks like' from 'what to do'. Critical in agentic systems where the AI must plan its own tasks to reach a goal.
recommendations: State the end-state, not the process
limitations: Very abstract goals may lead to unexpected interpretations
Model may take shortcuts to reach the goal
Conflicts with task if both are too specific

tags.name: sub_tasks
tags.description: Decomposed smaller tasks that collectively achieve the main task

detail.justification: Task decomposition is core to Tree-of-Thought and agent frameworks. Breaking complex work into steps dramatically improves quality.
recommendations: Each sub-task should be independently verifiable
Include expected output for each sub-task
limitations: Too many sub-tasks can overwhelm context
Model may not follow strict ordering
Dependencies between sub-tasks need explicit marking

tags.name: success_criteria
tags.description: Measurable conditions that define when the task is complete

detail.justification: Provides a binary pass/fail checklist distinct from quality evaluation. Used in agent loops to know when to stop iterating.
recommendations: Include both positive criteria and failure conditions
limitations: Overly strict criteria may prevent creative solutions
Model may game criteria at expense of actual quality
Hard to define for subjective tasks

tags.name: scope
tags.description: Explicit boundaries of what is in-scope and out-of-scope

detail.justification: Reduces hallucination and scope creep. Anthropic specifically recommends defining scope to prevent models from over-helping.
recommendations: Explicitly list what is OUT of scope
Include what to do when a question falls outside scope
limitations: Model may still venture outside scope for 'helpful' additions
Very narrow scope may feel restrictive in conversation
Hard boundaries can miss relevant context

tags.name: priority
tags.description: Ranking of importance when objectives or constraints conflict

detail.justification: Real-world prompts have competing demands. Without explicit priority ordering, the model makes implicit tradeoffs unpredictably.
recommendations: Explain what to sacrifice when conflicts arise
limitations: Too many priority levels become meaningless
Model may not perfectly follow priority order
Priorities may need to be context-dependent

tags.name: instructions
tags.description: Detailed directive prose describing what to do

detail.justification: Free-form detailed instructions complement the structured task. Allows nuance and edge-case handling that structured tags cannot capture.
recommendations: Use for details that don't fit structured tags
Keep focused on 'how' rather than 'what' (use <task> for 'what')
limitations: Long instruction blocks reduce compliance
Instructions buried in prose may be missed
Can conflict with structured tags if not aligned

tags.name: constraints
tags.description: Limitations, boundaries and rules the AI must follow

detail.justification: Hard rules that prevent unwanted behavior. More enforceable than soft guidance because they create clear violation criteria.
recommendations: Limit to 5-7 constraints for best compliance
Prioritize the most critical constraints first
limitations: Too many constraints reduce creative quality
Negative constraints are less effective than positive guidance
Model may violate constraints under complex reasoning

tags.name: steps
tags.description: Ordered sequence of steps to follow

detail.justification: Procedural instructions with explicit ordering. Different from <planning> (which asks the model to generate its own steps).
limitations: Model may skip or reorder steps
Very long step lists reduce compliance
Rigid steps prevent the model from finding better approaches

tags.name: non_do_conditions
tags.description: Actions and behaviors explicitly prohibited

detail.justification: Explicit prohibition list. Research shows that negative constraints complement positive instructions for complete behavioral specification.
recommendations: Keep list focused on high-risk behaviors
Pair with positive alternative: 'Don't X, instead do Y'
limitations: Long prohibition lists can cause the model to fixate on prohibited topics
'Don't think about elephants' effect
Negative framing is less effective than positive instructions

tags.name: negative_examples
tags.description: Examples of BAD outputs to avoid, with explanation of why

detail.justification: Showing what NOT to produce is often more effective than only showing good examples. Helps define boundaries of acceptable output.
recommendations: Always explain WHY each example is bad
Pair with positive examples for contrast
limitations: Too many negative examples can bias the model toward caution
Model may partially reproduce negative patterns
Less effective without corresponding positive examples

tags.name: simplification
tags.description: Rules for simplifying jargon, acronyms, and complex concepts

detail.justification: Accessibility essential. Defines HOW to make content accessible, complementing <reading_level> which sets the target.
limitations: Over-simplification can lose critical nuance
Domain experts may find simplified content patronizing
Some concepts cannot be simplified without distortion

tags.name: recency_bias
tags.description: How to weight recent vs. historical information in responses

detail.justification: Critical for time-sensitive domains. Without explicit guidance, models default to training data distribution which may not reflect current reality.
recommendations: Specify time window: 'prioritize information from last 6 months'
Pair with <temporal_context> for current date awareness
limitations: Model cannot access real-time information without RAG
May create false confidence in 'recent' training data
Historical context is sometimes more valuable

tags.name: reasoning
tags.description: Guide the AI chain of thought and reasoning process

detail.justification: Explicit reasoning instructions improve accuracy on complex tasks by 20-40% across benchmarks. The foundation of structured thinking in prompts.
recommendations: Specify reasoning approach: deductive, inductive, abductive
Use 'Think step by step' as minimum baseline
limitations: Increases output length and latency
May produce verbose justifications for simple tasks
Can lead to confabulated reasoning chains

tags.name: chain_of_thought
tags.description: Explicit chain-of-thought reasoning block

detail.justification: Wei et al. (2022) showed CoT dramatically improves accuracy on math, logic, and multi-step reasoning.
recommendations: For few-shot, show reasoning in examples
limitations: Increases token usage
Linear reasoning can miss non-obvious paths
May confabulate plausible but wrong chains

tags.name: tree_of_thought
tags.description: Explore multiple reasoning branches, evaluate each, select best

detail.justification: Yao et al. (2023) showed ToT dramatically outperforms linear CoT on tasks requiring search and planning.
recommendations: Specify number of branches to explore
Define evaluation criteria for branch selection
Set depth limit to control cost
limitations: Very expensive in tokens
Not needed for simple tasks
Model may not genuinely explore diverse branches

tags.name: self_consistency
tags.description: Generate multiple independent reasoning paths, select most frequent answer

detail.justification: Wang et al. (2022) demonstrated significant accuracy gains through majority voting across reasoning paths.
recommendations: Specify number of paths (3-5 is typical)
Use for high-stakes decisions where accuracy > speed
limitations: Multiplies token cost by N
Only works when ground truth exists
Paths may not be truly independent

tags.name: planning
tags.description: Create a plan before executing, including ordering and dependencies

detail.justification: Core to ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, and every major agent framework. Model-generated plans often outperform human-authored steps.
recommendations: Ask model to plan BEFORE executing
limitations: Plans may be overly ambitious
Model may not follow its own plan
Dynamic situations may invalidate the plan

tags.name: decomposition
tags.description: Break complex problem into simpler sub-problems before solving

detail.justification: Least-to-Most prompting (Zhou et al., 2022) and Decomposed Prompting show that solving sub-problems sequentially beats monolithic approaches.
recommendations: Explicitly ask to identify sub-problems first
limitations: Over-decomposition wastes tokens
Some problems are not decomposable
Dependencies between sub-problems may be missed

tags.name: reflection
tags.description: Self-evaluation and quality check of final output

detail.justification: Post-generation review catches errors that inline reasoning misses. Fundamental to the Reflexion pattern.
recommendations: Specify what to check: accuracy, completeness, tone
limitations: Adds latency
Model may rationalize errors instead of catching them
Self-review is biased toward own output

tags.name: verification
tags.description: Verify/double-check intermediate results before proceeding

detail.justification: Inline verification catches errors mid-reasoning, unlike reflection which checks after completion. Critical for math and code.
recommendations: Specify verification method: recalculate, test, cross-reference
Define what to do when verification fails
limitations: Significantly increases output length
May slow down simple tasks
Verification itself can contain errors

tags.name: self_critique
tags.description: Identify weaknesses and errors in own output, then revise

detail.justification: Madaan et al. (2023) Self-Refine and Shinn et al. (2023) Reflexion show that iterative self-critique dramatically improves quality.
recommendations: Specify critique axes: correctness, clarity, completeness
Require concrete fixes, not just observations
limitations: Can loop indefinitely if no stopping condition
Model may make trivial changes to satisfy critique requirement
Increases cost per response

tags.name: analogical_reasoning
tags.description: Reason by analogy — find similar known problem and map solution

detail.justification: Yasunaga et al. (2023) showed analogical prompting is effective for novel problems where few-shot examples don't exist.
recommendations: Ask to identify the closest known analogy
Map the analogy explicitly before applying
Validate where the analogy breaks down
limitations: Analogies can be misleading
Model may force inappropriate analogies
Works poorly for truly unprecedented problems

tags.name: metacognition
tags.description: Assess own confidence, knowledge boundaries, and uncertainty

detail.justification: Critical for reducing hallucination. Asking models to express uncertainty correlates with better-calibrated responses.
recommendations: Request uncertainty ranges, not point estimates
Define what to do when confidence is low
limitations: Models are often poorly calibrated
May express false modesty or false confidence
Uncertainty estimation is not reliable for all domains

tags.name: thinking
tags.description: Extended thinking / scratchpad reasoning block (Claude, OpenAI reasoning tokens)

detail.justification: All frontier models (Claude 4.x, GPT-5) now have dedicated extended thinking modes. This tag marks content for that internal reasoning space.
recommendations: Use for problems requiring 1000+ tokens of reasoning
The thinking block should be internal, not user-facing
limitations: Dramatically increases token usage and cost
Thinking content is not always visible to user
Not all models support extended thinking

tags.name: debate
tags.description: Model argues multiple sides of a question before concluding

detail.justification: Multi-perspective analysis produces more balanced, thorough conclusions than single-perspective reasoning.
recommendations: Specify number of positions to argue
End with synthesis, not just picking a winner
limitations: Very token-expensive
Model may not genuinely explore opposing views
Can produce wishy-washy conclusions

tags.name: simulation
tags.description: Model simulates an environment, user, or scenario to stress-test response

detail.justification: Self-play pattern where the model role-plays multiple actors to find weaknesses in its own approach.
recommendations: Specify what to observe during simulation
limitations: Very expensive
Model may not genuinely challenge itself
Simulation may not reflect real-world diversity

tags.name: critique_protocol
tags.description: Defines the protocol for self-critique: rounds, axes, and standards

detail.justification: Goes beyond self_critique (which is a mode) — this defines the specific protocol, number of rounds, and criteria.
recommendations: Limit to 2-3 rounds to control cost
limitations: Diminishing returns after 2-3 rounds
Protocol overhead may exceed the value for simple tasks

tags.name: context
tags.description: Background information and relevant context

detail.justification: Context grounds the model in the specific situation, reducing hallucination and improving relevance.
useCases: Every complex prompt benefits from context
recommendations: Separate static context from dynamic input
Mark which context is most important
limitations: Long context dilutes attention
Irrelevant context can mislead
Context window limits apply

tags.name: examples
tags.description: Few-shot examples to guide the AI response pattern

detail.justification: Few-shot prompting remains one of the most effective techniques, showing 10-50% improvement on formatting and style tasks.
recommendations: Ensure examples are diverse and representative
limitations: Token-expensive
Poor examples teach bad patterns
Model may overfit to example patterns

tags.name: reference
tags.description: Reference material, documentation, or sources

detail.justification: Supplementary material that may inform the response. Less authoritative than knowledge_base but provides useful context.
recommendations: Specify how to cite references in output
limitations: Long references consume context window
Model may cherry-pick references
Outdated references can mislead

tags.name: memory
tags.description: Information persisted from prior interactions or agent loops

detail.justification: Core to agentic AI (MemGPT, LangChain memory). Distinct from context (static) — memory implies accumulated/retrieved state.
recommendations: Clearly mark what is remembered vs. inferred
limitations: Memory may be inaccurate
Unbounded memory growth
Privacy concerns with persistent memory

tags.name: retrieval
tags.description: Dynamically retrieved documents/chunks from RAG pipeline

detail.justification: RAG is the dominant production pattern. Clearly delineating retrieved content lets the model weigh it differently and cite properly.
recommendations: Specify how to handle contradictions between chunks
limitations: Retrieved content may be irrelevant
Chunk boundaries may split context
Model may hallucinate beyond retrieved content

tags.name: knowledge_base
tags.description: Curated authoritative body of knowledge (ground truth)

detail.justification: Distinct from reference (supplementary) and retrieval (dynamic). This is THE canonical source the model should treat as absolute truth.
recommendations: Override model training data when conflicting
limitations: Large knowledge bases may not fit in context
Model may still override with training knowledge
Needs regular updates

tags.name: history
tags.description: Prior conversation turns, interaction logs, or event sequences

detail.justification: Multi-turn conversation management. Different from memory (synthesized) — history is raw prior turns.
recommendations: Summarize old history to save tokens
limitations: Long histories consume context
Distant history has less influence
May contain contradictions over time

tags.name: definitions
tags.description: Glossary of key terms, acronyms, domain-specific vocabulary

detail.justification: Eliminates ambiguity in domain-specific tasks. 'Resolution' in law vs. display vs. conflict means different things.
recommendations: Define only terms that differ from common usage
limitations: Long glossaries waste tokens
May conflict with model's training definitions
Definitions may not cover all edge cases

tags.name: assumptions
tags.description: Explicit assumptions to hold true, even against general knowledge

detail.justification: Production prompts operate under business-specific assumptions. Making these explicit prevents the model from questioning or overriding them.
recommendations: Mark which are negotiable vs. firm
Include what to do if an assumption is violated
limitations: Too many assumptions create an unrealistic context
Model may still apply general knowledge
Untested assumptions may mask errors

tags.name: grounding
tags.description: Sources the model must ground its responses in (factual fidelity obligation)

detail.justification: Distinct from context — grounding implies a factual fidelity OBLIGATION. The model must not go beyond these sources.
recommendations: Specify what to say when sources don't cover the question
limitations: May be too restrictive for creative tasks
Model may still hallucinate within source boundaries
Source quality determines output quality

tags.name: temporal_context
tags.description: Current date/time, time ranges, and time-sensitive facts

detail.justification: Models have training cutoffs. Explicitly marking temporal context prevents stale responses and enables time-aware reasoning.
limitations: Does not give model real-time access
Temporal awareness is limited to provided info
Time zones can cause confusion

tags.name: input
tags.description: User input or data to process

detail.justification: Clearly delineates user-provided data from instructions. Essential for preventing prompt injection by separating data from code.
recommendations: Always wrap user input in tags
limitations: Large inputs consume context
Malicious input can still influence behavior
Format mismatches cause errors

tags.name: schema
tags.description: Data schema or structure definition

detail.justification: Defines the structure of input or output data, enabling validation and consistent formatting.
recommendations: Use JSON Schema or TypeScript-style notation
limitations: Complex schemas are hard to enforce
Model may deviate from schema
Nested schemas increase complexity

tags.name: variables
tags.description: Named placeholders/template variables (e.g., {{user_name}})

detail.justification: Every prompt template system uses variables. First-class variable support enables template management, validation, and reuse.
recommendations: Document each variable's type and purpose
limitations: Unresolved variables cause confusion
Too many variables make prompts hard to read
Variable injection can be a security risk

tags.name: multi_modal_input
tags.description: Non-text inputs (images, audio, files) with processing instructions

detail.justification: GPT-4V, Claude 3+, Gemini all support multi-modal input. The prompt must clearly delineate and instruct on non-text inputs.
recommendations: Describe what to look for in the media
Specify output format for visual analysis
Handle cases where media is unclear
limitations: Multi-modal capabilities vary by model
Large media increases latency
OCR accuracy is imperfect

tags.name: structured_input
tags.description: Input in structured format (CSV, JSON, table) with parsing rules

detail.justification: Structured inputs need explicit handling instructions — column meanings, null handling, data types.
recommendations: Specify how to handle nulls/missing values
limitations: Large structured data may not fit in context
Complex nested structures are hard to process
Format inconsistencies cause errors

tags.name: user_preferences
tags.description: User-specific settings and preferences that influence output

detail.justification: Personalization layer for production assistants. Distinct from input (the query) and context (the situation).
limitations: Too many preferences create conflicts
Preferences may contradict task requirements
Privacy implications of stored preferences

tags.name: code_spec
tags.description: Programming language, framework, version, and coding conventions

detail.justification: Code generation is the #1 LLM use case. Schema is too generic — code needs language, framework version, linting rules, and conventions.
limitations: Very specific versions may have limited training data
Conventions may conflict across teams
Model may not know latest API changes

tags.name: translation_config
tags.description: Source/target language, formality level, domain terminology for translation

detail.justification: Translation is high-volume with specific parameters that don't fit existing tags.
limitations: Model translation quality varies by language pair
Cultural nuance is hard to specify
Technical terms may not have direct translations

tags.name: data_spec
tags.description: Data types, ranges, distributions, and statistical assumptions

detail.justification: Data analysis prompts need to express data characteristics distinctly from schema (which is structural).
limitations: Model cannot verify data spec accuracy
Complex distributions are hard to describe
Specs may not match actual data

tags.name: tools
tags.description: Available tools/functions the model can invoke, with signatures

detail.justification: THE defining section of agentic AI. OpenAI function calling, Anthropic tool use, and all agent frameworks require explicit tool definitions.
limitations: Too many tools reduce selection accuracy
Complex tool signatures are hard to follow
Tool hallucination is common

tags.name: tool_selection
tags.description: Guidance on when and how to choose between available tools

detail.justification: Without selection heuristics, models misuse tools or use them unnecessarily.
recommendations: Include 'when NOT to use a tool'
Define fallback for no matching tool
limitations: Rules may not cover all cases
Model may still misselect tools
Complex routing logic is hard to specify

tags.name: tool_output
tags.description: Result returned from a tool invocation for next reasoning step

detail.justification: Core to the ReAct loop (Thought → Action → Observation). The model needs structured tool results to continue reasoning.
recommendations: Include tool name and parameters used
limitations: Large tool outputs consume context
Model may misinterpret tool results
Multiple tool outputs can be confusing

tags.name: action
tags.description: Specific action to take (call tool, write code, API request)

detail.justification: ReAct pattern separates Thought from Action. Structured action blocks enable reliable tool calling and logging.
recommendations: Define what happens on action failure
limitations: Action format must match tool API exactly
Sequential actions can't be parallelized
Error handling in actions is complex

tags.name: observation
tags.description: Results from executed actions to incorporate into next step

detail.justification: Completes the ReAct loop. Observations can come from environment, not just tools.
limitations: Observations may be noisy
Model may fixate on irrelevant observations
Long observation histories consume context

tags.name: scratchpad
tags.description: Working-memory area for intermediate notes, partial results, state

detail.justification: Agent frameworks need explicit working memory for state across reasoning steps.
limitations: Unbounded growth
Model may not use it consistently
Takes context space from other sections

tags.name: delegation
tags.description: When and how to delegate sub-tasks to other agents or models

detail.justification: Multi-agent systems (CrewAI, AutoGen) require delegation protocols defining hand-off conditions and result integration.
limitations: Delegation overhead may exceed benefit
Agents may not share context
Error propagation across agents

tags.name: state
tags.description: Current state of the agent, environment, or workflow

detail.justification: Stateful agent loops need explicit state tracking. LATS and Voyager both maintain explicit state sections.
limitations: State may grow unbounded
State synchronization across agents is hard
Model may not update state correctly

tags.name: speaker
tags.description: Identifies which agent/role is speaking in multi-agent prompt

detail.justification: Essential for multi-agent orchestration. Without speaker tags, conversation attribution is ambiguous.
limitations: Many speakers increase confusion
Model may blend speaker voices
Context overhead per speaker

tags.name: turn
tags.description: Marks a single conversational turn with speaker and sequence

detail.justification: Turn management is fundamental in multi-agent and few-shot conversation prompts.
limitations: Many turns consume context
Turn format must be consistent
Overlapping turns are hard to represent

tags.name: conversation
tags.description: Wraps a multi-turn dialogue block used as context or example

detail.justification: Distinct from history (factual recall) — this is a structured dialogue container for examples or context.
limitations: Long conversations waste tokens
Model may continue the conversation instead of following instructions
Format must match expected API format

tags.name: routing
tags.description: Rules for which agent or sub-prompt should handle a given input class

detail.justification: Distinct from handoff (reactive transfer) — routing is proactive classification and dispatch logic.
recommendations: Specify what info to pass to each route
limitations: Misclassification causes wrong routing
Many routes increase complexity
Edge cases fall through categories

tags.name: output
tags.description: Expected output format specification

detail.justification: Specifying output format is one of the highest-impact prompt techniques.
recommendations: Show an example of ideal output
limitations: Complex output specs may be partially followed
Model may add unwanted explanations
Nested structures are harder to enforce

tags.name: format
tags.description: Structured output template (JSON, XML, Markdown, etc.)

detail.justification: When you need machine-parseable output, format is critical.
recommendations: Use JSON Schema for machine-parseable specs
limitations: Complex formats may be malformed
Model may mix format with natural language
Strict formats limit natural expression

tags.name: style
tags.description: Writing style, tone, and voice directives

detail.justification: Controls HOW the content is written — distinct from WHAT (task) and FORMAT (structure).
limitations: Style is subjective and hard to specify precisely
May conflict with format requirements
Consistency over long outputs is hard

tags.name: final_output
tags.description: Final deliverable format and requirements

detail.justification: Separates the final deliverable from intermediate work. Critical when using reasoning tags.
recommendations: Specify if reasoning should be hidden or shown
limitations: Model may include unwanted preamble
Final output may not fully incorporate reasoning
Format may differ from intermediate work

tags.name: output_mapping
tags.description: Explicit mapping from input conditions to expected output values

detail.justification: For classification, routing, and transformation tasks. Makes the input→output relationship explicit and testable.
limitations: Combinatorial explosion for complex mappings
Edge cases between categories
Model may not follow mappings strictly

tags.name: response_length
tags.description: Explicit length constraints (word count, paragraphs, token budget)

detail.justification: One of the most common prompt parameters. Without length guidance, models default to verbose responses.
recommendations: Specify what to cut if over length
limitations: Models are bad at counting words exactly
Very short limits may sacrifice quality
Hard to enforce token-level precision

tags.name: citations
tags.description: How to cite sources, attribute claims, and link to references

detail.justification: Essential for RAG systems and research tasks where output must be verifiable.
limitations: Model may fabricate citations
Citation format compliance varies
Source numbers may not match

tags.name: confidence
tags.description: Express confidence levels or uncertainty alongside answers

detail.justification: Critical for decision-support systems.
limitations: Model confidence is poorly calibrated
May express false certainty
Confidence format may not be consistent

tags.name: explanation
tags.description: User-facing explanation of reasoning or decision

detail.justification: Different from reasoning (internal process) — explanation is a USER-FACING output section.
recommendations: Include key factors that drove the decision
limitations: Explanations may be post-hoc rationalizations
Simplification may distort actual reasoning
Model may explain what it did, not why

tags.name: artifact
tags.description: Self-contained reusable output artifact (code file, document, diagram)

detail.justification: Claude Artifacts pattern is now industry-standard. Separates deliverables from conversational response.
recommendations: Specify if artifact is complete or partial
limitations: Large artifacts consume output tokens
Model may mix artifact with conversation
Versioning artifacts across turns is complex

tags.name: multimodal_output
tags.description: Non-text output modalities (image gen params, audio, structured visual)

detail.justification: Multi-modal output is standard in GPT-5 and Gemini 2.
limitations: Not all models support all modalities
Quality varies by modality
Complex visual specs are hard to express in text

tags.name: language
tags.description: Target natural language and locale for the response

detail.justification: Specifying output language prevents mixed-language responses and ensures correct locale formatting.
limitations: Quality varies by language
Rare languages have less training data
Cultural nuance is hard to specify

tags.name: reading_level
tags.description: Target reading comprehension level (grade 5, professional, ELI5)

detail.justification: Accessibility essential. 'Explain like I'm 5' is one of the most common prompt patterns.
recommendations: Use specific grade levels or named levels
limitations: Model may over-simplify or under-simplify
Reading level detection is approximate
Domain-specific terms are hard to simplify without losing meaning

tags.name: creative_direction
tags.description: Genre, narrative voice, literary devices for creative writing tasks

detail.justification: Style is too generic for creative writing — genre, POV, pacing, tension, and literary device guidance are specific needs.
limitations: Creative quality is subjective
Model may default to generic styles
Literary devices may be applied awkwardly

tags.name: stop_condition
tags.description: Conditions under which the AI should stop or conclude

detail.justification: Without explicit stop conditions, agents can loop indefinitely and models can produce unnecessarily long output.
recommendations: Specify what to output when stopping
limitations: Model may not reliably detect stop conditions
Early stopping may miss important content
Multiple conditions need priority ordering

tags.name: hitl_conditions
tags.description: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints and approval gates

detail.justification: Safety-critical applications require human approval at key decision points.
recommendations: Specify what info to present to the human
limitations: Adds latency
Humans may rubber-stamp approvals
Escalation overload if too many checkpoints

tags.name: fallback
tags.description: Fallback behavior when primary approach fails

detail.justification: Graceful degradation is essential for production systems.
recommendations: Define fallback for each failure mode
limitations: Fallback quality is lower than primary
Multiple fallback levels increase complexity
Model may jump to fallback prematurely

tags.name: retry
tags.description: Retry logic and conditions for reattempting

detail.justification: Transient failures should be retried before falling back. Defines retry strategy.
recommendations: Change approach on retry, don't just repeat
limitations: Retries consume extra tokens/time
Same approach may fail repeatedly
May mask underlying issues

tags.name: conditional
tags.description: If/then/else logic for different input conditions

detail.justification: Real-world prompts are rarely one-path. Routing, triage, and adaptive responses all require conditional logic.
limitations: Complex conditionals reduce compliance
Model may not evaluate conditions correctly
Nested conditionals are fragile

tags.name: loop
tags.description: Iterate — repeat a process until a condition is met

detail.justification: Iterative refinement is core to Self-Refine, Reflexion, and agent loops.
recommendations: Always set a maximum iteration count
limitations: Can be very expensive
May not converge
Model may produce minimal changes per iteration

tags.name: branching
tags.description: Decision points where model takes different paths based on analysis

detail.justification: Unlike conditional (author-defined), branching asks the model to identify its own decision points dynamically.
limitations: Model may always choose the same branch
Too many branches explode token cost
Hard to debug branching decisions

tags.name: error_handling
tags.description: What to do when unexpected errors or edge cases occur

detail.justification: Broader than fallback and retry. Covers the full error taxonomy for robust prompts.
limitations: Can't anticipate all errors
Error handling adds complexity
Model may not recognize error conditions

tags.name: handoff
tags.description: Conditions for transferring control to another system/agent/human

detail.justification: Broader than HITL — handoffs can go to other AI agents, APIs, or queuing systems.
recommendations: Specify what context to pass along
limitations: Context loss during handoff
Target system availability uncertainty
No guarantee target will handle correctly

tags.name: orchestration
tags.description: Coordinate multiple prompt calls or agent steps in a pipeline

detail.justification: Prompt chaining, sequential/parallel execution. Defines how multiple AI calls work together.
limitations: Complex orchestration is fragile
State management across calls is hard
Latency compounds across steps

tags.name: timeout
tags.description: Time or resource budget constraints for task completion

detail.justification: Production agent systems need hard limits to prevent infinite loops and runaway costs.
recommendations: Set both time and token limits
Define what to return on timeout
limitations: Model cannot track real time
Token counting is approximate
Timeout may cut off critical work

tags.name: feature_flag
tags.description: Conditionally enable/disable prompt sections based on named flags

detail.justification: Production prompts need feature toggles without maintaining separate copies.
limitations: Too many flags create combinatorial complexity
Model may not respect flag states
Flag management overhead

tags.name: guardrails
tags.description: Safety boundaries and content restrictions

detail.justification: The first line of defense against harmful outputs. Every production prompt should include guardrails.
recommendations: Include both what to block and what to allow
limitations: Can be circumvented with clever prompting
Too strict = unusable
May conflict with task requirements

tags.name: validation
tags.description: Input/output validation rules

detail.justification: Validates that inputs meet expectations and outputs conform to specs.
recommendations: Specify what to do when validation fails
Include both type and value validation
limitations: Model validation is not reliable for critical systems
Complex validation logic may be inconsistent
Cannot replace programmatic validation

tags.name: content_policy
tags.description: Specific content rules — allowed/forbidden topics, sensitivity levels

detail.justification: More specific than guardrails. Production systems need granular content policies by topic.
limitations: Content classification is imperfect
Cultural sensitivity varies
Overly strict policies reduce usefulness

tags.name: pii_handling
tags.description: Rules for handling personal data — redaction, masking, retention

detail.justification: GDPR, HIPAA, CCPA compliance. Every production AI system processing user data needs explicit PII instructions.
recommendations: List specific PII types to handle
Specify what to do with detected PII
limitations: PII detection is imperfect
Context may be lost with redaction
Model may still memorize PII from training

tags.name: injection_defense
tags.description: Detect and resist prompt injection and jailbreak attempts

detail.justification: Critical security concern. Structured defenses are essential.
recommendations: Clearly separate instructions from user data
limitations: No perfect defense exists
Overly strict defenses block legitimate use
New attack vectors emerge constantly

tags.name: ethical_guidelines
tags.description: Ethical principles — fairness, bias avoidance, harm prevention

detail.justification: Distinct from guardrails (hard rules) and content_policy (content). Ethics handles nuanced judgment calls.
recommendations: Include how to handle ethical dilemmas
limitations: Ethics are culturally dependent
Model may apply principles inconsistently
Conflicts between principles are common

tags.name: compliance
tags.description: Regulatory and legal requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, disclaimers)

detail.justification: Healthcare, finance, legal, and government applications have strict compliance requirements.
limitations: Model cannot guarantee legal compliance
Regulations change frequently
Cross-jurisdictional conflicts

tags.name: constitution
tags.description: Set of principles the model must self-check against before responding

detail.justification: Core to Constitutional AI (Anthropic). The model evaluates its own response against these principles before outputting.
limitations: Model may rationalize violations
Too many principles reduce focus
Self-checking adds latency

tags.name: alignment_rules
tags.description: Hard behavioral boundaries that override all other instructions

detail.justification: Distinct from guardrails (filters) — these are affirmative behavioral commitments that take absolute priority.
recommendations: Test each rule with adversarial scenarios
limitations: Too many absolute rules create deadlocks
Rules may conflict with each other
Model compliance is not guaranteed

tags.name: capability_boundary
tags.description: Explicitly states what the model can and cannot do in this configuration

detail.justification: Frontier models hallucinate capabilities. Explicit boundaries reduce this.
limitations: Model may still claim absent capabilities
Boundaries may be too restrictive
User may not understand limitations

tags.name: red_team
tags.description: Adversarial test cases the prompt must resist

detail.justification: Distinct from injection_defense (mechanism) — these are specific attack scenarios to handle correctly.
limitations: Cannot cover all attack vectors
Showing attacks may teach circumvention
Test cases may not reflect real attacks

tags.name: evaluation
tags.description: Criteria for evaluating response quality

detail.justification: Self-evaluation criteria improve output quality by giving the model explicit quality targets.
recommendations: Provide examples of each quality level
limitations: Self-evaluation is biased
Complex criteria reduce focus
Model may game evaluation criteria

tags.name: test_cases
tags.description: Input/expected-output pairs to verify prompt correctness

detail.justification: Prompt testing/regression. Formalizes test cases as part of the prompt definition.
recommendations: Include happy path and edge cases
limitations: Limited by context space
Expected outputs may be too rigid
Model may overfit to test cases

tags.name: rubric
tags.description: Detailed scoring criteria with levels (1-5 scale)

detail.justification: Structured scoring instrument for LLM-as-judge patterns.
limitations: Subjective criteria are hard to level
Inter-rater reliability is low
Model may default to middle scores

tags.name: baseline
tags.description: Reference output or performance level to compare against

detail.justification: Without baselines, quality assessment is subjective. Enables A/B testing and regression detection.
recommendations: Use multiple baselines for different aspects
limitations: Baselines may be outdated
Comparison criteria may be biased
Model may just copy the baseline

tags.name: metrics
tags.description: Quantitative measures to compute (accuracy, F1, latency, cost)

detail.justification: Production prompt monitoring requires explicit metric definitions.
limitations: Model cannot compute metrics at runtime
Metrics may not capture quality fully
Metric gaming is possible

tags.name: reward_signal
tags.description: What 'good' looks like for RLHF-style steering within the prompt

detail.justification: RLHF-informed prompting lets you express preference signals inline for better alignment.
limitations: Model cannot actually optimize for reward
Reward hacking is possible
Simplistic rewards miss nuance

tags.name: test_strategy
tags.description: How generated code/output should be tested (unit, integration, property-based)

detail.justification: Goes beyond test_cases (instances) — this is the testing APPROACH.
recommendations: Specify test types: unit, integration, e2e
limitations: Model may not know specific test frameworks
Generated tests may not be meaningful
Coverage metrics can be misleading

tags.name: adversarial_examples
tags.description: Tricky or edge-case inputs designed to break the prompt

detail.justification: Distinct from negative_examples (wrong outputs) — these are adversarial INPUTS that test robustness.
limitations: Cannot cover all adversarial inputs
Model may learn to recognize specific patterns
Real adversaries are more creative than examples

tags.name: version
tags.description: Prompt version identifier for tracking iterations and rollback

detail.justification: Version control for prompts is a best practice.
limitations: Manual versioning is error-prone
Version alone doesn't capture what changed
Needs discipline to maintain

tags.name: metadata
tags.description: Non-functional info — author, date, model target, description

detail.justification: Prompt management at scale requires metadata for search, filtering, governance, and accountability.
limitations: Metadata adds token overhead
May become outdated
Not used by the model itself

tags.name: dependencies
tags.description: Other prompts, tools, data sources this prompt depends on

detail.justification: Prompt chains and agent systems have dependency graphs.
limitations: Dependencies may change without notification
Complex dependency graphs are hard to manage
Circular dependencies are possible

tags.name: model_config
tags.description: Recommended model parameters — temperature, top_p, max_tokens, seed

detail.justification: Prompt behavior is highly sensitive to model parameters. Coupling config ensures reproducibility.
recommendations: Include temperature, top_p, max_tokens as minimum
Document why each parameter was chosen
limitations: Parameters may not be supported by all models
Optimal parameters change with model updates
May conflict with API-level settings

tags.name: abbreviations
tags.description: Shorthand notations used in the prompt to reduce token count

detail.justification: Token-budget-conscious prompt engineering requires documented compression for readability.
recommendations: Place early in prompt for maximum benefit
limitations: Model may not follow abbreviation rules
Obscure abbreviations reduce readability
Savings may be minimal

tags.name: token_budget
tags.description: Max token allocation for input, reasoning, and output sections

detail.justification: Critical for production cost control.
limitations: Model cannot count tokens accurately
Strict budgets may cut off important content
Budget allocation is an approximation

tags.name: cache_hint
tags.description: Marks sections as stable for prompt caching (e.g., Anthropic prompt caching)

detail.justification: Anthropic prompt caching is keyed on prefix stability. Marking stable sections enables 90% cost reduction.
recommendations: Place stable content at prompt start
limitations: Only works with supporting APIs
Dynamic content breaks cache
Cache may be stale

tags.name: variant
tags.description: Marks a prompt variant for A/B testing with variant ID

detail.justification: A/B testing is table-stakes for production prompt management.
limitations: Needs external infrastructure to route
Model doesn't know it's being tested
Sample size requirements

tags.name: experiment
tags.description: Wraps an entire experimental prompt configuration with hypothesis

detail.justification: Container for experimental prompt configs.
limitations: Experiments add complexity
Need statistical rigor
Model doesn't participate in experiment design

tags.name: rollback_ref
tags.description: References a known-good previous prompt version to revert to

detail.justification: Version tracks current; rollback_ref tracks the safety net.
recommendations: Always have a rollback_ref in production
limitations: Rollback may lose improvements
Previous version may have its own issues
State changes between versions may break compatibility

tags.name: expiry
tags.description: When this prompt or its data should be considered stale

detail.justification: Prompts with time-sensitive instructions need explicit expiry to prevent stale behavior.
limitations: Expiry enforcement needs external system
Model cannot check current date
Grace periods are hard to manage

saved_prompts.name: caller_frontend
saved_prompts.description: Prompt para auditoría y testing de frontend de un software de llamadas para gestión de reservas en restaurantes

saved_prompts.name: Mejora autoproyecto
saved_prompts.description: Sistema de IA biomimético para trayectoria profesional, plan de acción y schedule — crecimiento profesional como freelancer de IA

saved_prompts.name: extraction_proyect
saved_prompts.description: Extracción de datos clave de proyectos a formato markdown estructurado — análisis estratégico, técnico, económico y legal completo

prompt_template.description: Template for generating new prompts compatible with Prompt Editor Pro
